sourceMovie.title: Lifeforce
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Lifeforce (1985) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pandorum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lifeforce clicked for you, Pandorum (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Oblivion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lifeforce clicked for you, Oblivion (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lifeforce?
faq.answer: Try Pandorum, Oblivion, Event Horizon—each shares DNA with Lifeforce while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lifeforce?
faq.answer: Lifeforce (1985) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction, Action, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lifeforce worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
